title: Calamus clemensianus
taxonomy: Calamus clemensianus is classified within the family Acoraceae, which is a small family of flowering plants commonly known as the arum family or calla family. The genus Calamus is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia, and it includes several species of climbing palms. Calamus clemensianus is a species of climbing palm that is native to the Philippines. It is a member of the Arecaceae subfamily Calamoideae, which includes various species of climbing palms. Calamus clemensianus is further classified as a species within the genus Calamus.

intro: Calamus clemensianus, also known as Indian calamus, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Zingiberaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Calamus clemensianus include compounds such as sesquiterpenes, particularly clemensianine and calamusol, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Calamus clemensianus is often used to prepare teas, decoctions, and tonics, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
